# Elder Care

**Confidence Level:** Low

# Executive Summary

> [!NOTE]
> Elder Care is a multi-market private-pay home care platform serving ~595 clients across NY, NJ, MA, and CT, with reported net revenue of $35.1M and PF adjusted EBITDA of $9.2M (19.9% margin) for TTM Aug-24, supported by strong client tenure (57% with 4+ years) and a differentiated offshore staffing model, but requiring close scrutiny of $11.3M in combined revenue adjustments, a reported EBITDA of only $2.8M, and an addback stack representing ~247% of reported EBITDA.



**Strengths**

- Demonstrated organic revenue growth from $9.0M (2020A) to $35.1M reported net revenue (TTM Aug-24), with PF adjusted revenue of $46.4M, reflecting consistent multi-year expansion across existing and newly entered geographies

- Strong client retention signal: 57% of active clients have been with the company 4+ years, and total client count grew 376% from 125 (Q1-20) to 595 (Q4-24E), indicating durable demand and low voluntary churn

- Differentiated hybrid staffing model with ~41% of the 108-person admin team based offshore (Philippines), enabling 24/7 caregiver support, on-call coverage, and scheduling at materially lower cost — a structural margin advantage relative to pure domestic operators

- Gross margins are stable and above-average for home care, ranging from 42.1% (2020A) to 43.4% (TTM Aug-24) on a reported basis, with service-level margins reaching up to 64% for RN nursing in select markets

- Seasoned executive team with relevant domain depth: CEO David Gilberg (8 years), SVP Sales Trish Paulson (15+ years in long-term care private pay), and COO Todd Abramson with prior scale-up experience at Santander and IBM

- 64% private-pay revenue mix reduces direct government reimbursement rate risk relative to Medicaid-heavy peers, and the weekly billing model ($1,927/client average) provides high revenue visibility at the individual client level

- Active inorganic growth strategy with two completed acquisitions in 2024 (Guided Living in MA, Unicity in NJ) adding 181 clients and expanding the geographic footprint to six markets



**Concerns**

- Reported EBITDA of $2.8M (7.9% margin, TTM Aug-24) is materially below the PF adjusted figure of $9.2M; the gap is bridged by addbacks totaling approximately 247% of reported EBITDA, including forward-looking synergy and ramp-up adjustments ([N], [O], [P]) that are not yet realized and carry execution risk

- Combined revenue adjustments of approximately $11.3M ($9.6M diligence + $1.7M pro forma) represent ~32% of the PF adjusted revenue figure of $46.4M — the magnitude and composition of these adjustments require independent QofE validation before any reliance on adjusted figures

- Multiple internal financial discrepancies identified across CIM sections, including conflicting 2023A revenue figures ($33.7M vs. $34.2M), gross profit variances for TTM Aug-24 ($18.5M vs. $20.2M), and EBITDA margin denominator inconsistencies — these require reconciliation with source accounting records

- Reported EBITDA was negative or near-zero in three of the four historical years (2020A: -$342K, 2022A: $180K, 2023A: -$870K), suggesting the business has only recently achieved meaningful reported profitability and that the adjusted EBITDA trajectory may overstate normalized earnings power

- Government and quasi-government payor concentration (Medicaid Waiver 11%, VA 7%, LTC Insurance 11%) totals approximately 29% of stated revenue, with Medicare and Managed Care not separately broken out — actual government exposure may be higher and warrants payor-level reimbursement rate analysis

- Key operational KPIs are absent from the data room: caregiver turnover rate, active client census by period, AR aging by payor, referral source breakdown, and compliance/audit history are not documented — these are primary diligence inputs for a home care platform

- Two 2024 acquisitions (Guided Living, Unicity) are recent and not yet fully integrated; the PF EBITDA includes pre-acquisition results and forward synergy estimates for both, which have not been independently verified

- Director of Finance has only 1 year of tenure and Director of Patient Services only 6 months, representing potential gaps in financial controls and clinical oversight during a period of rapid multi-market expansion

## Revenue Model

| Attribute | Assessment |
|-----------|------------|
| Tag | repeat_services |
| Quality Flag | Yellow |


### Assessment

Elder Care provides coordinated home care services through a refined practice model. Revenue is driven by repeat, non-contracted but habitual home care engagements (care management, caregiver coordination, nursing services) billed hourly or daily across multiple payor types.

# 6. Quality of Revenue & Earnings

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Addback % of EBITDA | 246.9 |
| Tier Summary | The Elder Care TTM Aug-24 adjusted EBITDA bridge contains 17 addback line items totaling a gross positive adjustment substantially in excess of the $2,773K reported EBITDA, with all 17 items classified Tier 4 due to the absence of any supporting document references across the entire addback schedule. The most material concerns are the $1,454K of unverified pre-acquisition earnings for Guided Living and Unicity (addbacks J and K), the $1,529K of speculative pro-forma maturity and synergy adjustments (addbacks N, O, P), and the $665K cash-to-accrual revenue conversion (addback D) which indicates the historical books were maintained on a cash basis — a finding corroborated by Guided Living's Schedule M-1 accrual-to-cash adjustments in its 2022 tax return. Revenue quality is further pressured by the addback quantum exceeding reported EBITDA by approximately 2.7x and the multi-payor mix including Medicaid Waiver and VA programs subject to reimbursement and eligibility risk. |

---

## Top Management Questions



1. Request and review Healthcare Referral Agreements *(priority: High, category: legal)*

2. What is the current total active client census by market/region, and how has it trended monthly over the past 24 months? *(priority: medium, category: kpi)*

3. What is the annualized caregiver turnover rate (voluntary and involuntary) by market for the past two fiscal years, and how does it compare to industry benchmarks? *(priority: medium, category: kpi)*

4. Can management provide a monthly payor mix breakdown by market for the TTM period, including any shifts in Medicaid Waiver or VA program concentration? *(priority: medium, category: kpi)*

5. Please provide an AR aging schedule segmented by payor class (Private Pay, Medicaid Waiver, LTC Insurance, VA) as of the most recent month-end, including DSO by payor. *(priority: medium, category: kpi)*

---

## Preliminary View — Closing


> [!IMPORTANT]
> Elder Care presents a home care platform with a compelling client retention profile, a differentiated cost structure, and a multi-year organic growth track record. The key open questions center on the quality and supportability of the adjusted financial figures — particularly the magnitude of addbacks relative to reported EBITDA, the revenue adjustment stack, and the realizability of acquisition synergies — as well as the absence of several standard operational KPIs that are material to assessing care quality, workforce stability, and payor risk. These items should be prioritized in confirmatory diligence before forming a view on normalized earnings power.
